message: Jake
Yuriko
Patricia
Gloria
Cindy
Kristy
Just an hour's flight from my home in New York,
there's Boston.
Right now, I'm at the mansion of Director Fabar, known as a Hollywood giant, invited by my best friend and Major League player Guy.
It seems the director has homes in both Hollywood and New York, but his original residence is this estate in Boston.
I remember reading somewhere that this is a place filled with memories of his late wife.
The spacious living room is fittingly splendid and beautiful, reflecting the mansion's grandeur.
And again, the invited people felt like celestial beings to me. I was intoxicated by this dreamlike reality.
Girl
Welcome today. It's Fabar's only daughter.
I'm.
Thank you for the invitation.
- Snipes.
Well, you’re the detective from New York!?
Surprisingly, she looked at me with an expression of delight as she said that.
Did Guy tell you that I’m a detective?
Of course! I was really looking forward to meeting you. It’s my first time meeting a real detective.
I felt like I wanted to hide if there was a hole.
When it comes to private detectives, it sounds good, but in reality, I was in a pathetic state having done hardly any meaningful work in three years since starting out.
Even the detective's license was something like half-bought with money.
Um.
Did you feel bothered about looking forward to meeting?
Huh, no, not at all.
It's an honor.
I'm glad to hear that. I was a bit anxious just now, thinking my expression was dark and I might have said something wrong.
Um, can you tell me about your job as a detective later?
Huh, oh, uh-huh.
You know, I actually admire detectives. I’d be so happy if I could get hired after I graduate.
Eh, w-well.
It's not like you can just say that out of the blue.
Please think about it! Well then, see you later.
While I was confused, she floated off with a satisfied smile to greet the other customers.
Even if I promised to tell her about the work of a detective, what could I possibly talk about?
Ah, this is a problem.
Ha.
Hey,　Boss!
We're in trouble, huh? Hehe.
To tease me, the secretary with a confused expression.
has arrived.
She’s a girl who’s quite the trend-follower, dreams of detective novels, and has a cute face but a bit of a difficult personality.
She must have,
likely been listening to every bit of my exchanges.
Boss, you look really
gloomy. You can’t have that face at the party!
Her smirking expression contradicts her words. It seems that people’s misfortune feeds her.
Anyway, you’ve probably heard everything with those Hell Ears, right?
Hehe.
Your ears are as big as Dumbo's. Ahem!
Oh, really, that’s good to hear.
Yes, they’re extremely useful.
For detectives, information gathering is an important point, so having a Hell Ear that can hear anything is an essential skill, right?
I-I suppose that's true?
No way, Boss. It's common sense!
What do you mean by common sense? I've known this girl for about a year, but I still can't grasp her thought process.
Could it be that I've already turned into an old man at 26?
By the way, I heard the main star Ted isn't coming today.
I'm disappointed that my target won't be here.
Sometimes I can't keep up with these sudden topic switches.
Who is Ted?
Huh?
You don't know Ted Grayson?
I can tell he's an actor, but unfortunately, I'm not into men.
When I said that,
he frowned intensely.
Is it really that infuriating not to know some guy named Ted?
Ah.
Oh, let's meet more wonderful people and have a meaningful time!
Right?
Ah,
Hi,
! How's it going? Are you having fun?
Hey.
What about you?
As you can see, Guy got taken by Director Fabar.
Oh dear, I'm sorry to hear that.
It's fine, really.
But,
did you imagine the director's house would be this amazing?
Not really. But,
isn't your house bigger?
No way, my home is a cozy, modest place.
Correction. I meant your parents' house, not where you live. There are easily 50 rooms, and of course, a pool.
Stop it! Don't bring up your parents' house at a time like this.
He clouded his beautiful face and glared at me.
Sorry.
I didn't know you cared so much.
I heard that you left home because of your parents' discord, but I never thought you hated home this much.
I'm the one who should apologize.
No, it's fine.
I was the one at fault.
She returned my weak smile.
Sorry, but I need some time alone.
And then she went that way.
I ruined the mood right after arriving.
It’s scary when she gets upset.
I have a bad feeling about this, but let’s hope Guy can smooth things over with her.
So, what should I do now?
Look around.
Talk　to　someone.
Think.
System.
The surrounding situation.
Tony.
Director Fabar.
Guy.
Tony.
Director Fabar.
Guy.
About　the　party.
About the movie.
About her.
About　him.
About Tony.
About　her.
About　him.
About Director Fabar.
About Guy.
About　her.
Save.
End the game.
My office seems spacious enough to fit six of them.
My mother is the daughter of one of the leading businesspeople in America, and when I was just a child, we went to her family home which had a room like this.
But since then, I've never come across a private residence with such a large room.
In New York, seeking this much space would practically mean it has to be an art museum.
I kind of feel like I've caught a glimpse of Hollywood.
Are you researching me?
Ask various things, does that mean something like that?
It could simply be curiosity, but if not, what purpose do you have in doing this?
Well.
It seems like they're having a good conversation. Since we're close in age, maybe that helps, but what on earth are they talking about?
If you look carefully, it seems like they're walking around everywhere, but I hope they don't cause any trouble.
She occasionally looks this way, but when our eyes meet, she blatantly looks away.
This has turned out badly.
Whenever I see her, it’s almost certain our eyes meet. She blushes shyly.
Am I being misunderstood in a strange way?
This is problematic.
I need to clear up any misunderstandings quickly, or I have a feeling I'll be in for a rough time later.
Her neat dress has resulted in an oddly striking presence in this situation.
In a good way, it attracts attention, but wouldn't it be better for the one hosting guests to not stand out?
It feels a bit too logical, but she seems like a straightforward and nice girl.
That's right.
There seem to be many bad rumors about him, but he doesn’t feel as terrible as they say.
They might actually be a lonely person.
They don't drop their seemingly arrogant attitude, but I wonder if that's some kind of occupational hazard.
It's concerning that they're standing alone but
Tightly.
They won't budge.
Is that guarding against the magic hands of Guy and Tonny?
I wonder if they are?
Are they that worried?
about it?
Standing close by is
Won.
She's the agent, a Chinese.
She has a somewhat unapproachable vibe.
She is certainly cute, and the fact that I could talk to her feels like a dream.
But, you know, maybe she's a bit too young.
As always.
She clings to me.
I can't help but feel a little pity.
Even from a distance, her shine is different. She really has something that sets her apart from regular girls.
Just being able to talk with her makes today’s party feel worthwhile.
Thinking about talking with him, he really seems like someone who has become an adult while keeping a boy's heart.
Even now, talking with Guy, he shows a smile like a kid who has just discovered something interesting.
He is indeed a big shot said to be a master in Hollywood, but he’s a friendly person.
He seems like a gentle director, but rumors say he has quite a harsh personality during work.
Still, I think it's normal for anyone to change a bit when it comes to work.
Director Fabar seems to be deep in conversation with Guy.
Guy is talking to the director again. Surely,
it's because he's avoiding someone.
Blatantly avoiding like that,
it's pitiful for her.
Guy is caught by the director.
I can’t hear what they are talking about, but it seems like they’re having a great time.
It’s not a big deal, but I wish they would.
talk to me a bit.
Standing all alone,
I wonder what’s going through their mind.
They were smiling bravely, but were clearly worried.
What the hell is Gai doing?
He’s left alone after the director took him away. However, he doesn’t show any dissatisfaction and brightens up the party.
By the way, they’re wearing a flashy dress today.
Sorry, but I can’t get away right now.
It’s my first time at a party like this, but do they often have gatherings before filming?
If Gai becomes an actor, will he have chances like this again?
It’s a rare opportunity to see a celebrity up close. I’m not a fanboy or anything, but I still feel my heart racing.
I’m feeling a bit uncomfortable, though.
It’s a movie about a story of a major league player that Gai is in, but the cast is quite luxurious.
Gathering such a glamorous cast seems like it would hit just from that, but that might just be a naïve thought.
I wonder what the budget is.
I wonder if I might get a chance to appear as an extra.
No way, right?
What were they asking about?
I don’t think it’s anything particularly weird, but if they want to know something, they could just ask me directly.
It’s hard to believe they see me with romantic feelings, so does that mean they need to investigate something about me?
Maybe I’ll probe a bit later.
It’s been a year since they became my assistant.
She is an inexperienced girl who has not yet reached the legal drinking age, but her earnestness dazzles me.
If I were to describe her in words, she’s a travel-loving, clumsy girl who’s into detective stories.
That’s about it.
What on earth bothered her?
I have no clue.
Without knowing the cause, I can't even try to cheer her up.
They say I'm incredibly dense, but...
What exactly am I not noticing?
Maybe,
she wanted to say she likes me?
Perhaps she’s just been raised in an isolated environment and hasn’t interacted with normal guys, which is why she's interested in someone like me.
The director's daughter
seems to be a bright, top student kind of girl.
That’s how it looks.
She might also be the type who likes to get one over on others, but that could be her defense mechanism.
Considering her age, she might be a bit on the younger side.
From her childhood spent mostly around adults, I imagine she’s gotten used to being pampered.
Anyway, I think she's a surprisingly formidable girl.
What became clear from our conversation is that he is a very lonely person.
Though he seems to live in a glamorous world, the reality is far from dazzling. Perhaps it's the fate of those who live on stage?
However, that look in his eyes is unsettling.
Tonny Ford.
He is not a director or actor about whom one hears good rumors.
Isn't the source of those heartless rumors his piercing gaze that sends chills down your spine?
I have no way of knowing what he thinks, but he gives off a feeling of carrying loneliness, which makes me feel sympathy.
I get that they really value it, but other than that, it feels like they don't care.
When someone is that obsessed, you can't help but wonder if there's something behind it.
They might be an excellent agent, but with that,
it must feel pretty cramped.
They stick so closely to them, as if they're guarding them.
It's clear they're protecting her from the guys.
I wonder if it's really appropriate to have that kind of attitude in a setting like this.
Maybe they're just being overly earnest.
I didn’t expect them to be so bubbly. It made me feel my age a bit.
But I think that vibe she has is crafted for business, and deep down, she's probably a lot smarter.
Am I just imagining it, or do I sense a lonely vibe from her?
Her outfit is basically a work uniform. Well, I suppose this party is part of her job.
They've been practically glued to the agent since earlier. Maybe she's the shy type?
I'm sure they're a scary director on set, but talking to them feels like chatting with a nice guy.
It's like they have a boyish heart, and I can't help but like them.
I guess you can't succeed as a film director without that kind of sensitive soul.
Even though I wasn't invited by Director Fabar this time, I bet Gai at least got their permission.
I need to thank them later.
They say the director is a scary person, but so far, I haven't seen a hint of it.
No way.
I hadn't noticed that my relationship with them had become fragile since I hadn't been in touch much lately.
Ah, I see.
It must be awkward for them to be just the two of them, so they invited me to the party.
I don't intend to interfere with their matters, but I genuinely hope things return to the way they were.
Guy is as cheerful as ever, which is enviable.
I think if I had that kind of cheerfulness, I could have lived my life without wasting time.
Still, it's unexpected to be invited to a party by Guy.
It takes effort to attend a party with Guy, who's not doing well, and keep a smile on my face.
He looked so happy that it didn't show.
I wonder if he's alright?
It's odd to call her my ex. Every time I meet her, I wonder if she's found a boyfriend; I need to get my act together so she doesn't feel embarrassed.
Well, I'm just being a bit too naggy.
It's nice that I'm close with Guy, but I feel a bit left out.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
Ｖ＝
Ｎ＝
